body.title: [sprint] Build-packet pre-inference compiler — measured, gated, fingerprinted (port Delta's 51% result to UU)
body.description:
UU already has pre_inference_assemble.py (pattern match + symbol map + domain terms, wired into sprint-ticket step 2.3). Delta (Akien's day job) went a step further: a deterministic build_packet.json (schema build.packet.v1, stage pre-inference) that PACKAGES intent + hard_constraints + success_definition + scored context_shortlist + sufficiency_gate + proof_plan + consequence_check BEFORE the LLM runs, with a baseline-vs-helper-first token measurement and a determinism fingerprint. A real isolated run measured 51.06% token reduction (709 -> 347). This ticket brings that fuller artifact to UU: extend the existing assembler into a measured, gated, fingerprinted build-packet so sprint-ticket emits one per ticket.

Real run result to reproduce/track:
  Baseline estimated tokens: 709
  Helper-first estimated tokens: 347
  Estimated reduction: 362 tokens
  Reduction percent: 51.06%
  Status: improved

The three things the current assembler lacks: (1) sufficiency_gate (pass/fail + missing_fields) that REFUSES to proceed when fields are missing — CP1 'I don't know' made structural; (2) proof_plan.token_measurement — baseline vs helper-first tokens measured per packet, proof-on-close as a first-class output; (3) determinism.fingerprint_sha256 — same input -> same packet (what makes it a compiler, not a prompt).

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/pre_inference_assemble.py (extend), new devlab/claudecode/build_packet.py (or module), skills/sprint-ticket/SKILL.md (emit/consume the packet at step 2.3), tests/test_build_packet.py (new). TBD: exact token-estimator source.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (flat-file/JSON artifact only), test-plan-or-why-not, proof-on-close (the token-measurement IS the proof lever), docs-live-in-code.
**Scope boundary:** IN — build_packet.v1 artifact shape, sufficiency_gate, token measurement (baseline vs helper-first), determinism fingerprint, sprint-ticket wiring, tests. OUT — the inference-proxy/Archivist graph-lookup layer (separate, T-archivist-device); cross-device packet sharing; auto-blocking the sprint on a failed gate (surface, don't block, in v1).
**Test plan:** test_build_packet.py — (a) same input produces identical fingerprint (determinism); (b) sufficiency_gate returns fail + names missing_fields when a required field is absent; (c) token_measurement reports baseline > helper-first on a fixture ticket (reduction > 0); (d) packet validates against build.packet.v1 schema. Proof: a red->green where pre-impl emits no measurement / no gate and post-impl emits both.

## Fable consult wiring note (2026-07-04): the packet must ABSORB the signature map, not compete with it. CodingDomain._initial_message (domains/coding.py) currently prepends build_signature_map() as a separate orientation block; when this ships, the packet's context_shortlist becomes THE single orientation artifact and replaces that prefix (feed the shortlist from query_file_symbols in scraps/orientation_classifier.py, which already returns multiple symbols per file). One orientation artifact, not two. GATED behind the editor-isolation smoke test (T-ds-editor-isolation-smoke-test): do not invest in compiling the packet until DS.0 is shown able to apply a known-good plan into Edit calls at all — the packet is the architect's INPUT, worthless if the output half can't serialize edits.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
